what_it_does: Defines a React full-screen modal image editor that previews and bakes crop, tone, filter, rotation, and flip edits to a canvas data URL, with an AI-edit tab that sends the currently baked image to a global OpenAI image-edit helper.
observations: Uses aliased React hooks from the global React object rather than imports.
Maintains edit state locally for mode, tone controls, hue, rotation, flips, filter, normalized crop rectangle, drag state, AI prompt, AI busy state, and AI results.
Builds a combined CSS filter string from a preset filter plus brightness, contrast, saturation, and hue controls.
Bakes edits by loading image.src into an Image, drawing the selected crop to a canvas with transform and filter applied, then returning a JPEG data URL.
Crop UI uses a normalized 0..1 rectangle and document-level mousemove/mouseup listeners during drag.
AI edit flow bakes the current image, converts it to a blob via fetch, then calls window.cvOpenAI.editImage.
Renders three modes: Adjust, Crop & rotate, and AI edit.
standouts: Canvas baking combines crop, rotation, flips, CSS filters, and tone controls into a single JPEG data URL.
AI edits operate on the current baked edit state rather than the untouched source image.
Crop manipulation is implemented with normalized coordinates and draggable move/corner handles.
conventions: Uses global component and service access such as CvIcon, ViShape, window.cvOpenAI, window.dsaToast, and window.cvNav.
Uses cv-ed prefixed class names for editor-specific UI and dsa-btn classes for shared button styling.
Uses small local helper functions inside the component for editor actions.
Uses optional callback invocation for onSave and onClose in some places.
Uses range inputs through a local Slider component.
concerns: runAiEdit and render-time button conditions call window.cvOpenAI.isConfigured() directly, so a missing window.cvOpenAI object would throw.
Crop drag deltas are based on the stage bounding rectangle, which may not match the displayed image bounds if the stage has padding or different dimensions.
The fine rotation slider allows arbitrary angles, but the bake canvas dimensions only account for quadrant-style width and height swapping, so non-90-degree rotations can be clipped.
bake draws image.src to a canvas with crossOrigin set; remote images without compatible CORS headers can still taint the canvas and prevent toDataURL.
The crop overlay coordinates are aligned to the image wrap while drag math is tied to the stage, which makes the coordinate basis ambiguous from this file alone.
notes: A self-contained modal editor that treats local visual adjustments, crop geometry, and AI image editing as one image-editing workflow.
questions: Is window.cvOpenAI guaranteed to exist before this component renders?
Should fine-angle rotation preserve the full rotated bounding box instead of using cropped canvas dimensions?
Should crop drag calculations use the actual displayed image or overlay bounds rather than the stage bounds?
Does onSave expect only a data URL string, or should it also receive metadata such as dimensions and active edits?
purpose_vs_actual: The header comment matches the implementation: the file renders a full-screen image-editing sheet with crop, adjustments, filters, rotation, and OpenAI-backed AI edits, then returns a final image data URL through onSave.
intention_signals: The file seems intended to provide a reusable image-editing surface where manual visual changes and AI-generated variants can both feed back into the caller as a saved image.
novelty: It combines conventional crop and adjustment controls with a baked-image handoff to an AI edit service inside the same modal workflow.
contribution: This file contributes a reusable modal image editor pattern with local adjustment controls, normalized crop handling, canvas export, preset filters, rotation and flip controls, and an AI-edit branch that consumes the current edited image state.
summary_for_embedding: React modal image editor component with adjustment sliders, filter presets, normalized crop overlay, rotation and flip controls, canvas JPEG baking, and OpenAI-backed AI image edits.
intention_for_embedding: It was for giving imagery surfaces a shared editor that can preview manual edits, bake the result, and optionally generate AI-edited variants from the current image state.

symbols.does: Renders and manages the full-screen image editor modal.
symbols.description: This component owns the editor state, derives preview filter and transform strings, renders mode-specific controls, and coordinates save, crop, and AI edit behavior. It receives an image record plus onSave and onClose callbacks, and returns edited image data through those callbacks.

symbols.does: Resets tone, filter, rotation, and flip settings to defaults.
symbols.description: This helper restores brightness, contrast, saturation, hue, filter, rotation, and flip state to their initial values. It does not reset crop state or AI tab state.

symbols.does: Applies the current edit pipeline to a canvas and resolves a JPEG data URL.
symbols.description: This async helper loads image.src, calculates the source crop in natural image coordinates, creates a rotation-aware canvas, applies translation, rotation, flips, and CSS filters, then draws the cropped image. It resolves with canvas.toDataURL('image/jpeg', 0.92) or rejects on image load failure.

symbols.does: Bakes the current edits and passes the result to onSave.
symbols.description: This async helper calls bake and invokes onSave with the resulting data URL. If baking fails, it reports a short failure message through window.dsaToast.

symbols.does: Starts a crop drag operation for a crop box handle or move action.
symbols.description: This handler only acts in crop mode. It captures the stage bounds, pointer start position, selected corner or move operation, and a snapshot of the current crop rectangle into drag state.

symbols.does: Updates the normalized crop rectangle while dragging.
symbols.description: This document-level mousemove handler derives normalized dx and dy from pointer movement over the captured stage rectangle. It adjusts x, y, width, and height differently for each corner or for moving the whole crop, using clamp to keep the crop inside bounds and above a minimum size.

symbols.does: Ends the active crop drag operation.
symbols.description: This document-level mouseup handler clears drag state by setting it to null. It is installed only while a drag is active through the surrounding effect.

symbols.does: Constrains a numeric value between a low and high bound.
symbols.description: This helper returns the input value limited to the inclusive lo and hi range. It is used by crop drag logic to keep crop dimensions and positions valid.

symbols.does: Sets a centered crop rectangle for a requested aspect ratio.
symbols.description: This helper reads the natural dimensions of the displayed image, compares the requested ratio to the image aspect ratio, and calculates normalized crop width and height. It then centers that crop rectangle in image coordinates.

symbols.does: Sends the currently baked image and prompt to the AI image edit service.
symbols.description: This async helper checks OpenAI configuration, clears previous AI results, bakes the current edits, converts the data URL to a blob, and calls window.cvOpenAI.editImage with the prompt and image blob. It stores returned variants in aiResults and reports failures through window.dsaToast.

symbols.does: Renders a labeled range control with a value display and reset button.
symbols.description: This small component presents a label row, current numeric value with unit, reset button, and range input. It delegates value changes to the supplied onChange callback after converting the input value to a number.
